items.title: Accused killer denies murder but admits burning woman's body, court told

children.content: Johnathan Paul Parker allegedly told a friend that he had strangled his housemate, Krystal Marshall, and asked for help to dispose of her body, a court has heard.

items.title: More rain to follow overnight storm and 50k lightning strikes in parts of SA

children.content: Wet and wild weather is lashing parts of South Australia with the Bureau of Meteorology warning about wind risk during storms.

image.altText: People cross a pedestrian crossing while carrying umbrellas with a wet ground

items.title: Woman 'still here to see grandkids grow' welcomes free HIV treatment in SA

children.content: When Kath Leane was diagnosed with HIV almost 40 years ago, she feared she would never see her children — let alone her grandchildren — grow up. But the antiretroviral treatment which helped make that possible is today being made free for all South Australians.

image.altText: A grandmother with long blond hair sits in an armchair in her home with a bookcase behind her

items.title: E-motorcyclist filmed doing wheelie, crashing while police attend another collision

children.content: SA Police publishes a video of a teenager on an electric motorcycle doing a wheelie and then crashing into a car while they were attending another collision at the same location in Adelaide's southern suburbs.

image.altText: Dashcam footage of crash site.

gallery.altText: A motorcycle rider doing a wheelie past commercial buildings

thumbnail.altText: A motorcycle rider doing a wheelie past commercial buildings

items.title: Patrons farewell historical pub in outback town amid spate of closures

children.content: There used to be more than 70 pubs in Broken Hill, but now only a handful remain. The Australian Hotels Association warns hotels in remote locations need to diversify their business model in order to survive a rapidly changing hospitality landscape.

image.altText: An older man standing outside a country pub with a grimace on his face, as it rains in the background

items.title: Volunteers say National Trust of SA warned them against speaking publicly

children.content: Volunteers who spoke to the media say they felt "threatened" with formal warnings from the National Trust of South Australia. They are now preparing to share their experiences in a parliamentary inquiry.

image.altText: A woman with cropped white hair and a necklace in a living room

items.title: 'One last crack' to restart Whyalla’s blast furnace as workers face nervous wait

children.content: The aging blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks could be permanently shut down before the steelworks transitions to a direct-reduced iron plant and electric arc furnace, placing hundreds of jobs at risk.

image.altText: A man wearing an orange jacket and white hard hat stands outside a large industrial building

items.title: 'No genuine opportunity' to prevent crash that killed two women, coroner finds

children.content: A South Australian coroner says it is an "unfortunate truth" that there was no way for police to predict or prevent a collision that killed a senior police officer and another woman in 2020.

image.altText: An image of Joanne Shanahan and Tania McNeill.

items.title: Thunderbirds co-captain to lead 'connected' team in grand final repeat

children.content: The Adelaide Thunderbirds are looking to win their third premiership in four years, with co-captain Georgie Horjus putting their success down to strong team connections on and off the court.

image.altText: Three women playing netball in a stadium

items.title: Midwife hit with $70,000 foreign investment tax after moving to Australia

children.content: A British midwife who migrated to Australia to work at a regional hospital during statewide staff shortages is calling for changes to the law after she was slugged $70,000 by state and federal authorities for the purchase of her home.

image.altText: A British midwife with brown hair stands next to her husband in front of their home in Whyalla

items.title: Rex Airlines breached continuous disclosure obligations

children.content: Rex contravened its continuous disclosure obligations, misleading the market, the NSW Supreme Court found on Tuesday.

image.altText: a plane flies in the sky

items.title: Temporary ban to protect Whyalla's 'significantly low' cuttlefish numbers

children.content: From Wednesday, a 90-day exclusion will begin at the cuttlefish sanctuary zone between Black Point and Stony Point in South Australia's Upper Spencer Gulf.

image.altText: two large cuttlefish near rocks underwater

items.title: In Australia, if you can't play the property game you lose

children.content: If you've been wondering how and why Australian house prices spun out of control you're not alone. One filmmaker was so depressed by it he went out and shot a documentary.

articles.title: Housing price downturn accelerates as 'perfect storm' hits market

children.content: Australian home values record their biggest decline in more than three years as the housing market slowdown that began in Sydney and Melbourne spreads across the country.

items.title: Quick hits: Mexico madness at the Azteca, big Salah injury update

children.content: Mexico fans enjoy a night to remember at the Azteca, Egypt gives a big update on Mo Salah's fitness, and the Netherlands lose their manager after a shootout disaster. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

articles.title: French unity on display as their ruthless run through World Cup continues

children.content: One person has died and multiple people have overdosed across Victoria, leading to the discovery of heroin in substances thought to be methamphetamine or cocaine.

items.title: Fraud trial hears of businesses paid $30k for hiring Chinese nationals

children.content: Wagga Wagga businessman Alan Eldridge tells the NSW District Court he received a $30,000 payment for employing a Chinese national through an initiative introduced to him by former NSW MP Daryl Maguire.

image.altText: A man in a dark jacket, white shirt and tie leaves a building with a woman and man.

items.title: Authorities renew safety warning as Neil the seal destroys barrier

children.content: Neil the seal has become one of Tasmania’s biggest celebrities, with social media videos showing him knocking over bollards and leaning on cars.
But wildlife experts are concerned his popularity could put him and the public at risk.
Some large animals have been euthanised overseas due to public safety concerns, but Tasmanian authorities say that option is not part of their management approach.

image.altText: An elephant seal on top of a broken fence

items.title: Who is Maya Joint, Australian conqueror of Serena at Wimbledon?

children.content: Maya Joint was born in April 2006 when Serena Williams had already won seven Grand Slam singles titles. So who is the 20-year-old who just defeated a tennis legend in her return to the All England Club?

articles.title: Why Maya Joint had to catch a glimpse of Serena Williams before their match

children.content: Australian Maya Joint had to catch a glimpse of Serena Williams in the days leading up to their Wimbledon opener just to remove some of the mythos around the 23-time grand slam champion.

image.altText: Serena Williams and Maya Joint walk onto centre court at Wimbledon.

articles.title: Australian rising star takes down Serena Williams in Wimbledon return

image.altText: Maya Joint shakes Serena Williams's hand over the net at Wimbledon.
